title: 10 best herbal creams for arthritis

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with arthritis because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound found in turmeric. Curcumin has been shown to reduce joint pain and swelling by inhibiting the production of enzymes that cause inflammation. Additionally, it has been found to improve joint mobility and reduce stiffness. By applying turmeric cream directly to affected joints, it can provide targeted relief and help alleviate symptoms of arthritis.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Turmeric's active compounds, including curcumin, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help reduce joint pain and inflammation associated with arthritis.

Cyclooxygenase inhibition: Turmeric's ability to inhibit the production of cyclooxygenase enzymes helps reduce the body's production of inflammatory prostaglandins, leading to decreased joint swelling and pain in individuals with arthritis.

Antioxidant activity: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect joints from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development and progression of arthritis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of turmeric contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce joint pain and inflammation associated with arthritis.

Roots: Turmeric roots have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various inflammatory conditions, including arthritis, due to their potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including curcuma longa powder 250mg, coconut oil 100ml, beeswax 20g and essential oils.
Melt beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until fully liquefied.
Add curcuma longa powder to the melted beeswax stirring well to combine for 1-2 minutes.
Mix in coconut oil slowly allowing it to emulsify with the beeswax mixture for 5-7 minutes.
Pour the cream into a container and let cool to room temperature before sealing and storing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with arthritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, are absorbed through the skin and into the affected joints, where they reduce swelling and relieve pain. Additionally, ginger has been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are chemicals that cause inflammation in arthritis sufferers. By reducing inflammation, herbal ginger creams provide relief from joint stiffness, swelling, and discomfort associated with osteoarthritis, making daily activities more manageable.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols, shogaols, and paradols that help reduce inflammation in the joints, which can alleviate pain and stiffness associated with arthritis.

Analgesic and pain-relieving properties: Herbal ginger creams also contain analgesics that can help relieve pain and discomfort caused by arthritis, allowing for increased mobility and reduced reliance on prescription medications.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in herbal ginger creams can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which may contribute to the development of chronic conditions like arthritis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, rhyzomes are a key ingredient in many arthritis creams due to their ability to reduce joint pain and inflammation.
Roots: The roots of the ginger plant contain sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory properties, making them an effective addition to arthritis creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh ginger roots and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the ginger roots in a warm and dry place for 3-4 days to remove excess moisture.
Grind 100g of dried ginger into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered ginger with 50ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil and mix well for at least 30 minutes.
Transfer the mixture to an air-tight container and store it in a cool dark place for 2 weeks before using it on skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort creams helps with arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The herb contains active compounds that reduce pain and swelling in the joints, providing relief from the discomfort associated with arthritis. Additionally, St John's wort has antioxidant properties that help to protect the joints from further damage, promoting joint health and flexibility. Regular application of a cream containing St John's wort can help to alleviate symptoms of osteoarthritis, rheumatoid arthritis, and other types of inflammatory arthritis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: St John's Wort cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as hyperforin and adhyperforin, can reduce swelling and pain in joints affected by arthritis.

Pain relief: The creams' active ingredients, including flavonoids and terpenes, have analgesic properties that help alleviate joint pain and stiffness associated with conditions like osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis.

Antioxidant activity: St John's Wort cream's antioxidant properties can help protect joints from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to the development of arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate joint pain, making them an effective ingredient in arthritis creams.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and hyperforin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can soothe arthritic joints and reduce symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves from a trusted source and clean thoroughly under running water.
Dry the hypericum perforatum leaves in a well-ventilated area or using a dehydrator at low temperature for 24 hours.
Grind the dried hypericum perforatum leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder and sift through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any lumps.
Mix 2 tablespoons of grated beeswax with 6 ounces of coconut oil in a double boiler, heating gently over low heat for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally.
Add 1 teaspoon of the powdered hypericum perforatum leaves to the melted mixture and stir well before pouring into sterile glass jars for storage at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow creams helps with arthritis because they contain salicin, a natural compound similar to aspirin that reduces inflammation and relieves pain. The topical application of white willow cream allows for direct absorption into affected joints, providing fast-acting relief from arthritis symptoms such as stiffness, swelling, and tenderness. As the cream penetrates the skin, it targets the source of the discomfort, providing a natural and effective way to alleviate joint pain and improve mobility.
remedy_properties: Salicin content: White willow creams contain salicin, a natural compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain in the joints.
Anti-inflammatory activity: The flavonoids present in white willow cream have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and joint stiffness associated with arthritis.
Antioxidant activity: White willow cream's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to joints, thereby reducing the progression of arthritis.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with arthritis.
Roots: The roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat rheumatism and arthritis due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality salix alba bark and dry it properly for at least two weeks to prevent degradation.
Grind 50 grams of dried salix alba bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered salix alba bark with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil at a ratio of 1:5.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heatproof bowl set over simmering water for 30 minutes to facilitate extraction of active compounds.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and allowing it to cool before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw creams helps with arthritis because they contain harpagoside, a bioactive compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This natural ingredient is able to target the underlying causes of arthritis pain and stiffness by reducing inflammation in the joints. Additionally, devil's claw creams have been shown to improve joint mobility and flexibility, making it easier for individuals with arthritis to perform daily activities without discomfort or pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Devil's claw creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like harpagoside, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in joints affected by arthritis.
Analgesic properties: The analgesic properties of devil's claw help to numb the affected area, providing temporary relief from pain and discomfort associated with arthritis.

Pain-relieving glycosides: Devil's claw creams contain glycosides like harpagide, which have been shown to have potent pain-relieving effects by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which contribute to inflammation and pain in arthritic joints.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in harpagosides, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing joint pain and swelling.
Rhyzomes: Contain a unique combination of saponins and phenolic compounds that help reduce inflammation and improve joint mobility.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried harpagophytum procumbens root and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground root with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 60°c to 70°c.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to room temperature before whipping it with a hand mixer or whisk for 5 minutes to create a creamy texture.
Fill sterilized glass jars with the cream, adding a label with the ingredients and instructions for use, and store them in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper creams helps with arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce joint pain and swelling. The cream's active ingredients, such as capsaicin, work by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain. Additionally, bell pepper creams contain antioxidants that help to protect joints from damage caused by free radicals. By applying the cream directly to affected areas, individuals can experience relief from arthritis symptoms, including stiffness and reduced mobility.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal bell pepper creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like capsicum and cucurbitacin E, which help reduce inflammation and pain in joints associated with arthritis.

Antioxidant activity: Bell peppers are rich in antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, which help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of chronic diseases like arthritis.

Pain-relieving compounds: Herbal bell pepper creams contain pain-relieving compounds like capsaicin, which binds to receptors in the skin and nerves, blocking the transmission of pain signals and providing relief from joint pain associated with arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Bell pepper roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds like kaempferol and quercetin, which help reduce joint inflammation and alleviate arthritic pain.
Seeds: Bell pepper seeds have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them a valuable component in natural arthritis creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh capsicum annuum leaves and stems at dawn for optimal potency and minimal bitterness.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dry place for 7-10 days to preserve bioactive compounds.
Combine one part dried capsicum annuum with two parts coconut oil and four parts beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully incorporated and melted.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood creams helps with arthritis because they contain thujone, a natural compound that has potent anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to affected joints, wormwood creams can reduce swelling and alleviate pain caused by inflammation. The cream's anti-inflammatory agents also help to reduce joint stiffness and improve range of motion, making it easier to move and perform daily activities without discomfort. Additionally, wormwood's antiseptic properties may help prevent infections that can exacerbate arthritis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Wormwood creams contain flavonoids, lignans, and sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce swelling, pain, and stiffness in joints associated with arthritis.

Pain-relieving agents: The terpene content in wormwood, including sesquiterpenes like artemorin, has been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, providing relief from arthritis-related pain.
Antioxidant activity: Wormwood's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to joint damage and exacerbate symptoms of arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which provide anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing joint pain and inflammation.
Barks: Contain a high concentration of salvinorin A, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce swelling and alleviate arthritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality artemisia absinthium herb and beeswax and start preparing the ingredients.
Combine 20g of dried artemisia absinthium leaves with 100ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 4 hours in a cool, dark place to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth and mix it with 20g of melted beeswax in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture until smooth and creamy, then transfer it to a glass container for storage and use as needed within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with arthritis because its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties provide relief from joint pain and stiffness. The natural oils in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, work to reduce swelling and relax the muscles surrounding the affected joints. Additionally, lavender's calming effects can help alleviate stress and anxiety, common co-morbidities with arthritis that can exacerbate symptoms. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, English lavender creams offer a soothing and effective natural remedy for managing arthritis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: English lavender contains anti-inflammatory compounds like linalool, linalyl acetate, and camphor, which help reduce inflammation and pain associated with arthritis.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in English lavender cream, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to joint degeneration and arthritis.

Analgesic and anxiolytic properties: Lavender's analgesic and anxiolytic compounds, like linalool, have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress-related pain, making it easier for people with arthritis to manage their symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lavender roots contain a rich source of calcium, magnesium, and potassium, which can help alleviate joint pain and inflammation associated with arthritis.
Buds: Lavender buds are high in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may reduce swelling and ease discomfort caused by arthritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the ground lavender powder with 200ml of cold-pressed coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 100ml of distilled water to the mixture and stir well before increasing the heat to medium and simmering for 20 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool down to room temperature before straining the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter for 5 minutes.
Pour the strained mixture into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator at 10°c for up to 6 months after labeling with the date and ingredients.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with arthritis because they contain a unique blend of herbal extracts that work synergistically to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties, derived from the Tasmanian blue gum tree, help to soothe sore joints and muscles, while its natural analgesics provide rapid relief from discomfort. Additionally, the cream's emollient base helps to hydrate and nourish the skin, further enhancing its therapeutic benefits and promoting a sense of comfort and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Eucalyptol and other terpenoids in Tasmanian Blue Gum cream have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with arthritis.
Pain-relieving properties: The oil extracted from Tasmanian Blue Gum leaves contains camphor, a natural analgesic that can help ease joint pain and stiffness caused by arthritis.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in Tasmanian Blue Gum cream may help protect joints from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to the development and progression of arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian blue gum contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce joint swelling and alleviate pain associated with arthritis.
Barks: The barks of the Tasmanian blue gum have been traditionally used to make medicines that relieve symptoms of arthritis, such as inflammation and stiffness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry eucalyptus globulus leaves in a warm air circulation drying oven for 2 hours at 60°c.
Grind 50g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix the ground eucalyptus globulus powder with 250ml of sweet almond oil and 10g of beeswax in a double boiler for 1 hour.
Heat the mixture to 60°c while stirring constantly for 15 minutes, then let it cool and set at room temperature for 2 hours.
Whip the cooled mixture using an electric mixer or blender until smooth and creamy, yielding 300ml of eucalyptus globulus cream in approximately 1 hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cayenne creams helps with arthritis because they contain capsaicin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound found in chili peppers. When applied topically to affected joints, capsaicin reduces pain and inflammation by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain. This relief is especially effective for patients suffering from osteoarthritis, rheumatoid arthritis, and fibromyalgia, as it targets the source of discomfort and helps restore mobility and flexibility.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Cayenne creams contain capsaicin, which has potent anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce swelling and pain associated with arthritis.
Pain relief: Capsaicin in cayenne cream binds to nerve endings, blocking the transmission of pain signals to the brain, providing temporary relief from chronic joint pain.
Vasodilation: Cayenne's active compounds help dilate blood vessels, increasing circulation and reducing inflammation, which can help alleviate stiffness and soreness associated with arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the cayenne plant contain a high concentration of capsaicin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and stiffness associated with arthritis.

Rhizomes: Rhizomes are the underground stems of the cayenne plant, and they have a higher concentration of bioactive compounds like capsaicinoids, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal creams for arthritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh capsicum frutescens leaves and stems from organic farms.
Dry the harvested material in low-temperature ovens at 60 degrees celsius for 12 hours.
Grind the dried capsicum frutescens into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered capsicum frutescens with 20% beeswax and 80% coconut oil in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth to remove any impurities and transfer it to glass jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense creams helps with arthritis because they contain boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that effectively reduce joint pain and swelling. These natural compounds also improve mobility by reducing stiffness and inflammation in the joints. Additionally, frankincense has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory molecules, further alleviating arthritic symptoms. By incorporating frankincense creams into a daily routine, individuals with arthritis may experience significant relief from discomfort and improved joint health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Frankincense contains boswellic acids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with arthritis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant: Frankincense cream has antioxidant properties that help protect joints from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing joint degeneration and improving overall joint health.
Analgesic: The boswellic acids in frankincense also have analgesic (pain-relieving) effects, providing relief from chronic pain associated with arthritis without causing habituation or addiction.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Frankincense rhyzomes contain boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing joint pain and inflammation associated with arthritis.
Barks: The barks of frankincense contain a compound called oleo-gum-resin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, helping to soothe and calm arthritic joints.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried boswellia serrata resin and grind it into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the powdered boswellia resin with 100 grams of beeswax and 200 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, for 30 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted.
Add 20 drops of vitamin e oil and 10 drops of frankincense essential oil to the mixture, stirring well to combine.
Remove from heat, let cool, and pour into sterilized containers, allowing the cream to set at room temperature for 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with arthritis because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain. The antioxidants and essential oils present in sage have been shown to ease stiffness and improve joint mobility, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating the symptoms of osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis. By applying topical sage cream directly to affected joints, individuals can experience a reduction in pain and discomfort, allowing them to enjoy improved mobility and flexibility.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sage cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate joint pain associated with arthritis.

Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage joints and exacerbate arthritis symptoms, promoting overall joint health and reducing oxidative stress.

Pain-relieving flavonoids: The flavonoids present in sage, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, providing relief from arthritis-related pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing joint pain and swelling associated with arthritis.

Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the sage plant are rich in bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and improve joint mobility, making them a valuable ingredient in medicinal creams for arthritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves in the morning when essential oils are most concentrated.
Dry the leaves by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels for two days and then store in airtight containers.
Combine 20 grams of dried salvia officinalis with 40 grams of coconut oil and heat the mixture at 60°c for one hour.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar discarding the solids and let it cool to room temperature.
Add 2 teaspoons of beeswax pellets to the cooled oil mixture and melt in a double boiler heating gently until fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce joint pain and swelling. The antioxidants present in rosemary also help to neutralize free radicals, preventing further damage to joints. Additionally, rosemary has been shown to increase blood circulation, allowing for better nutrient delivery and waste removal from the affected areas. This natural combination of benefits makes rosemary creams a popular alternative treatment for alleviating arthritis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary cream's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in joints, which can alleviate arthritis symptoms like pain and stiffness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as camphor and borneol, can help reduce joint swelling and pain by blocking the production of inflammatory enzymes and chemicals.

Pain-relieving properties: Rosemary cream's analgesic compounds, including cineole and bornyl acetate, can help numb the area and relieve chronic pain associated with arthritis, allowing for improved mobility and function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, rosemary roots are used to reduce joint pain and inflammation.
Barks: Containing phenolic acids and flavonoids, rosemary barks help to relieve arthritic pain and stiffness by reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the early morning when their essential oils are most concentrated.
Dry the harvested leaves using shade drying or air circulation methods within 24 hours of collection for optimal preservation.
Combine 20 grams of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 hours, stirring occasionally, allowing the herbal extract to infuse into the carrier oil.
Strain and filter the infused oil using cheesecloth or a coffee filter, then mix with emulsifiers and preservatives as needed before packaging.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with arthritis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol in peppermint oil relaxes tense muscles, reduces swelling, and relieves joint pain and stiffness. Additionally, peppermint's analgesic properties numb the affected area, providing quick relief from chronic pain associated with osteoarthritis, rheumatoid arthritis, and fibromyalgia. By reducing inflammation and easing discomfort, herbal peppermint creams provide a natural solution for managing arthritic symptoms, allowing individuals to regain mobility and flexibility.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Peppermint creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like menthol, which help reduce inflammation and relieve joint pain and stiffness associated with arthritis.

Pain-relieving glycosides: The glycosides present in peppermint creams, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to have potent analgesic properties, providing relief from chronic pain and discomfort caused by arthritis.

Antioxidant properties: Peppermint creams' antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and cell damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development and progression of arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant contain a high concentration of menthol and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with arthritis.
Barks: The barks of the peppermint plant have been traditionally used to make salves and ointments for relieving joint pain and stiffness, as they are rich in tannins and volatile oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or purchase organic dried peppermint.
Combine one cup of dried peppermint leaves with two cups of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil.
Steep the peppermint mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes then strain and discard solids.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil at a ratio of three parts oil to one part wax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler until the beeswax is fully melted and the cream reaches a consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with arthritis because they contain rosmarinic acid, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that targets specific enzymes responsible for joint pain and stiffness. This natural ingredient has been shown to reduce swelling and inflammation in joints, providing relief from arthritis symptoms such as pain, stiffness, and limited mobility. Additionally, lemon balm's calming properties can help soothe irritated skin and promote relaxation, further alleviating discomfort associated with arthritis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Lemon balm creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which helps reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with arthritis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in lemon balm cream, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can damage joints and surrounding tissues, thereby reducing oxidative stress and inflammation caused by arthritis.

Pain relief and relaxation: Lemon balm's essential oils, including citral and nerol, have a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps reduce muscle spasms and tension associated with arthritis pain, leading to improved sleep quality and reduced morning stiffness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots contain a high concentration of rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties and helps reduce joint pain and swelling.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (underground stems) of the lemon balm plant are rich in essential oils that help soothe arthritis-related inflammation and improve joint mobility.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves from a pesticide-free area and rinse them gently with distilled water.
Dry the leaves in a cool dark place or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Combine 250 grams of dried melissa officinalis leaves with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a saucepan over low heat for 2-3 hours to create a infused oil preparation.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and mix it with beeswax and vitamin e oil to create a smooth cream consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with arthritis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and pain in joints. The cream's active compounds, including saponins and flavonoids, penetrate deep into the skin to target affected areas. This natural remedy also contains antioxidants that help combat free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with arthritis. As a result, pot marigold creams provide relief from joint stiffness, redness, and discomfort, making it an effective and natural solution for managing arthritic symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce joint swelling and pain associated with arthritis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties in pot marigold cream work to neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to joints, thereby reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that contributes to arthritis development.
Emollient: The emollient properties of pot marigold cream help soothe and lubricate dry, inflamed skin and joints, providing relief from stiffness and discomfort often experienced with arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce joint pain and swelling associated with arthritis.
Seeds: Containing flavonoids and other phytochemicals, seeds are known for their analgesic and antispasmodic effects, making them effective in relieving arthritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers at peak bloom and dry them thoroughly in a warm dark place for 7-10 days.
Combine 20 grams of dried calendula flowers with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 4 hours to allow for optimal extraction of flavonoids and terpenes.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth and discard the solids before mixing it with an emulsifier like beeswax and vitamin e oil.
Pour the resulting cream into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with arthritis because they provide a soothing and anti-inflammatory relief to joints affected by osteoarthritis. The herbal extracts in marshmallow creams, such as white willow bark and ginger, work together to reduce joint pain and swelling. Additionally, the creamy texture of marshmallow creams can help to lubricate dry, stiff joints, making movement easier and more comfortable. This natural remedy can be especially beneficial for individuals who experience chronic arthritis pain and stiffness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal marshmallow creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like mucilages, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with arthritis by coating and protecting inflamed joints.

Emollient and soothing properties: The cream's emollient and soothing properties help to calm irritated skin and joints, reducing redness and swelling, and promoting relaxation and comfort in individuals with arthritis.

Antioxidant properties: Marshmallow herbal creams contain antioxidants that help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to joint pain and inflammation associated with arthritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and lubricate joints affected by arthritis, reducing inflammation and pain.
Buds: Contain high amounts of flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing joint pain and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried marshmallow root and grind it into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the ground marshmallow root with 100 milliliters of distilled water in a small saucepan and simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove any solids and discard the solids.
Mix the resulting liquid with 200 grams of beeswax and 50 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a double boiler and heat until melted.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and let it cool and solidify at room temperature before storing it.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm creams helps with arthritis because of its unique ability to soothe and protect joint tissue. The mucilage extracted from slippery elm bark forms a protective barrier on the skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with arthritis. Additionally, slippery elm's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and pain in the affected joints. By providing a gentle, non-irritating barrier, slippery elm creams can provide long-lasting relief for those suffering from arthritis, allowing them to move more comfortably and maintain their daily activities.
remedy_properties: Demulcent property: Slippery elm cream helps to create a protective barrier on the skin, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by arthritis, allowing for faster healing and pain relief.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in slippery elm, such as mucilages and phenolic acids, help to reduce swelling and pain associated with arthritis, making it an effective natural remedy for joint pain and stiffness.

Soother and moisturizer property: Slippery elm's soothing and moisturizing properties help to hydrate the skin, reducing dryness and irritation that can exacerbate arthritis symptoms, while also providing long-lasting relief from discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and moisturize skin irritated by arthritis, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Barks: The barks contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce joint pain and swelling associated with arthritis, making them effective in topical creams for pain relief.
remedy_recipe: Gather and dry 10-15 grams of fresh ulmus rubra bark in a well-ventilated area for at least two weeks.
Combine the dried ulmus rubra bark with 30ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 20-30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to create a decocotion.
Strain the decocotion through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving 10ml of the infused oil.
Mix 2% beeswax with 98% of the reserved infused oil in a double boiler and heat until the wax is fully incorporated and the mixture reaches a temperature of 180-190°f.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with arthritis because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and relax muscles and joints. The cream's active ingredients, such as valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, work to reduce swelling and pain by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause inflammation. This natural anti-inflammatory response helps to alleviate joint stiffness, pain, and discomfort associated with arthritis, allowing for improved mobility and reduced disability.
remedy_properties: Analgesic properties: Valerian contains compounds that help reduce pain and discomfort associated with arthritis, providing relief from joint inflammation and stiffness.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness in the joints, reducing pain and stiffness and improving joint mobility.
Muscle relaxant properties: Valerian's muscle-relaxing effects can help ease spasms and cramps associated with arthritis, promoting relaxation and reducing tension in the affected areas.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are rich in compounds like falcarinol and sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate arthritis symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Valerian rhizomes contain a high concentration of valerenic acid, a compound that has been shown to reduce pain and inflammation associated with arthritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry fresh valeriana officinalis roots to ensure maximum potency and effectiveness of the cream.
Combine one part dried valeriana officinalis root powder with nine parts carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for approximately 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the ingredients are fully incorporated and the mixture is warm but not hot.
Strain the cream through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove any solids and ensure a smooth texture, allowing it to cool completely before use.
Transfer the cooled cream to an air-tight container and store in the refrigerator for up to two weeks, using as needed to soothe arthritis symptoms.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for arthritis are topical treatments infused with natural ingredients, such as arnica, ginger, and turmeric, which soothe and calm inflamed joints. These creams help alleviate arthritis symptoms by reducing pain, stiffness, and inflammation. For example, creams containing capsaicin can relieve pain and discomfort in patients with osteoarthritis, while creams rich in omega-3 fatty acids from flaxseed or fish oil can reduce inflammation and promote healing. As a result, many people with arthritis experience improved mobility, reduced swelling, and enhanced overall quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat arthritis with herbal creams is to choose creams containing ingredients like capsaicin, arnica, and wintergreen. Capsaicin reduces pain by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain. Arnica and wintergreen have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate joint pain. Apply the cream directly to affected joints, following product instructions for maximum benefits.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to arthritis that are treated with herbal creams are fibromyalgia, tendinitis, and bursitis. These conditions cause chronic pain, inflammation, and stiffness in the joints and muscles. Herbal creams containing natural anti-inflammatory agents like capsaicin, arnica, and menthol can provide relief from these symptoms by reducing pain and swelling, promoting relaxation, and improving circulation.
